data.narrative:
Xtend, a Series B-stage defense and security company founded by Aviv Shapira, has raised $100M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comparable universe of 8 private-market peers in the Defense & Security sector at the same stage, with a range spanning $300M to $1.5B, alongside 2 public benchmarks (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS).

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis, using total raised as a proxy baseline, applies a 1.9x step-up multiplier typical for Series B advancement, yielding $190M. Peer-median analysis across the 8-company cohort—including Vannevar Labs, Dream Security, Harmattan AI, Code Metal, and Castelion—produces a median valuation of $1.1B, substantially higher than the stage-progression result. The median peer valuation reflects late-stage maturity within the Defense & Security segment.

Triangulating these two methods produces a valuation range of $162M (low) to $1.3B (high), with a median of $658M. The 8x spread between methods reflects material uncertainty around whether Xtend's capital efficiency and market positioning align with the peer median or track closer to stage-typical progression. Key variables for narrowing this range include revenue scale relative to peers, customer concentration in the defense vertical, and pathway to Series C financing. The peer-median approach assumes comparable competitive positioning; material differentiation would warrant adjustment.
